senses.definition: a Thai martial art that combines boxing with stand-up striking and clinching techniques

examples.x: He trains in Muay Thai for its effective striking techniques.
Muay Thai fighters use elbows and knees for close combat.
She's studying Muay Thai to improve her self-defense skills.
He's won several matches competing in Muay Thai tournaments.
Muay Thai is known as the "art of eight limbs" due to its use of fists, elbows, knees, and shins.

senses.definition: a combat sport that combines elements of boxing and kicking techniques

examples.x: She won her last kickboxing fight with a knockout.
Learning kickboxing improves both fitness and self-defense skills.

senses.definition: a French form of kickboxing that incorporates both punches and kicks
a dynamic style of kickboxing emphasizing diverse striking techniques, originating in Japan and known for high-level competitions

examples.x: He trained specifically for K-1 rules competitions.
K-1 allows for a wide range of striking techniques.
They watch K-1 fights for their fast-paced action.
She's aiming to compete in K-1 tournaments next year.

senses.definition: a quick, straight punch thrown with the lead hand to create distance

examples.x: His opponent struggled to defend against his fast jabs.
Her jab was quick and accurate, disrupting her opponent's rhythm.
The jab is an essential tool for controlling the pace of the fight.
The coach emphasized the importance of a stiff jab in boxing strategy.

senses.definition: (boxing) a straight punch thrown with the rear hand, usually aimed straight at the opponent's head or body

examples.x: He landed a powerful cross to his opponent's jaw.
Her coach emphasized the importance of a strong cross.
She dodged the cross and countered with a hook.
The boxer's cross knocked his opponent down.
The boxer's cross was his go-to move for finishing fights.

senses.definition: (boxing) a powerful punch thrown with a bent arm aimed at connecting with the side or front of an opponent's head or body

examples.x: Her hook to the body weakened her opponent's defenses.
His opponent's guard dropped, opening up an opportunity for a hook.
The hook is effective for generating power and finishing combinations.
He knocked out his opponent with a devastating left hook.
He threw a quick hook to counter his opponent's jab.

senses.definition: (boxing) a powerful punch thrown from below, aimed upward toward the opponent's chin or body

examples.x: Her uppercut to the chin stunned her opponent in the final round.
They practiced uppercuts on the heavy bag to develop strength and accuracy.
He delivered a devastating uppercut that knocked out his opponent.
He used an uppercut to counter his opponent's aggressive attack.
His opponent underestimated the power of his uppercut.

senses.definition: (boxing) a strike aimed at the opponent's torso area, typically the ribs or abdomen

examples.x: He landed a powerful body punch in the third round.
Body punches can wear down an opponent over time.
She's known for her quick combinations, including body punches.
He dodged the jab and countered with a body punch.
The crowd cheered as he connected with a series of body punches.

senses.definition: (Muay Thai) a powerful strike where the fighter pivots on one foot while swinging the other leg horizontally to hit the opponent with the shin or foot

examples.x: He landed a devastating roundhouse kick to his opponent's ribs.
His roundhouse kick is one of the most effective moves in Muay Thai.
She blocked her opponent's roundhouse kick with a well-timed defense.
The crowd erupted in cheers as he delivered a perfect roundhouse kick.
She practiced her roundhouse kick technique for hours every day.

senses.definition: a defensive technique in boxing to dodge punches and set up counterattacks

examples.x: He used a bob and weave to evade his opponent's jab.
The boxer's bob and weave opened up an opportunity for a counterpunch.
She's quick with her bob and weave, making her hard to hit.
The boxer's bob and weave kept him safe while waiting for an opening.
His bob and weave allowed him to slip punches and land effective counters.

senses.definition: to obstruct or hinder the progress or action of an opponent, typically in sports or competitive activities

examples.x: In basketball, the defender blocked the opponent's shot with a well-timed jump.
The goalkeeper blocked the striker's attempt to score a goal with a diving save.
The tennis player strategically blocked her opponent's powerful serve with a quick return.
In volleyball, the middle blocker successfully blocked the opponent's spike, earning a point for her team.
The boxer effectively blocked his opponent's punches, minimizing the impact of the attacks.

senses.definition: the strategic shifting or dodging of the head in boxing to avoid incoming strikes from an opponent

examples.x: His head movement helped him evade his opponent's punches.
Effective head movement is crucial for defense in boxing.
Poor head movement often leads to taking unnecessary hits.
He utilized his head movement to counter his opponent's jabs.
Her head movement skills made her a difficult target to hit.

senses.definition: a punching technique where a boxer throws a hook while moving backward or sideways to counter an opponent's attack

examples.x: The check hook caught his opponent off guard.
She used a check hook to evade her opponent's straight right hand.
He landed a perfect check hook to counter his opponent's jab.
Her coach emphasized the importance of timing when using the check hook.
His check hook was his go-to move when facing aggressive opponents.

senses.definition: a striking technique in kickboxing targeting an opponent's thigh or lower leg

examples.x: He weakened his opponent with a powerful low kick.
His low kick landed perfectly on the opponent's thigh.
Low kicks can be devastating in a kickboxing match.
She used a low kick to disrupt her opponent's balance.
He countered the low kick with a quick dodge.

senses.definition: the situation where the referee ends the fight early due to one boxer's inability to continue safely

examples.x: The fight ended in a stoppage in the third round.
The referee called for a stoppage after the heavy blow.
The doctor advised a stoppage after examining the fighter.
A stoppage saved him from further punishment.
The trainer requested a stoppage seeing his fighter in trouble.

senses.definition: a situation where one fighter delivers a punch or series of punches that disable their opponent of fighting in that match, leading to an immediate victory

examples.x: Her left hook resulted in a stunning knockout early in the match.
Knockouts are often the result of well-timed and powerful punches.
The referee stopped the fight after a brutal knockout in the third round.
She celebrated her victory after scoring a knockout against a tough opponent.
The crowd erupted in cheers following the dramatic knockout.

senses.definition: a powerful strike that makes an opponent unconscious or unable to continue the fight, resulting in an immediate victory for the delivering boxer

examples.x: Knockout punches can end a fight suddenly and decisively.
Her left hook resulted in a stunning knockout punch early in the match.
He won the fight with a devastating knockout punch in the final round.
Knockout punches are often the result of well-timed and powerful strikes.
The referee stopped the fight after a brutal knockout punch in the third round.

senses.definition: a situation that occurs when a referee stops a fight because one fighter is unable to continue due to injury

examples.x: His opponent's relentless attacks led to a technical knockout in the fifth round.
The ringside doctor called for a technical knockout due to a cut above the eye.
She won the match by technical knockout after dominating her opponent throughout.
The fight ended in a technical knockout when the referee determined the fighter could no longer defend himself.
He suffered a technical knockout loss after being knocked down multiple times in the fight.

senses.definition: a situation where two of the three judges score the bout in favor of one boxer, while the third judge scores it in favor of the other boxer

examples.x: The bout was highly competitive, leading to a split decision that surprised many fans.
The judges deliberated over the closely contested match before reaching a split decision.
She celebrated her win despite a split decision, acknowledging the tough fight her opponent put up.
The boxer expressed disappointment after losing by split decision in a hard-fought battle.
The crowd erupted in debate following the announcement of the split decision.

senses.definition: a training exercise where a boxer practices their technique, footwork, and movement without an opponent, often visualizing an imaginary opponent or scenarios

examples.x: Shadowboxing helps boxers improve their coordination and fluidity of movement.

senses.definition: a controlled practice session where boxers simulate a real fight, focusing on technique, timing, and strategy without aiming to knock out their opponent
the long-distance running or jogging done by boxers to build stamina and endurance

examples.x: He starts his day with roadwork to build endurance.
Roadwork is essential for improving a boxer's stamina.
He believes consistent roadwork gives him an edge in the ring.
She never skips her morning roadwork sessions.
She combines roadwork with other training for peak performance.

senses.definition: a defensive style where the boxer holds their gloves close to their cheeks to protect their face while maintaining mobility

examples.x: His peekaboo guard allowed him to slip and counter his opponent's punches effectively.
The boxer's peekaboo stance minimized the openings for his opponent to exploit.

senses.definition: a left-handed fighting position where the boxer's right hand and right foot are forward

examples.x: He prefers fighting from a southpaw stance.
She's effective at counterpunching in her southpaw stance.
fighters who can work with southpaw stance often have an advantage against orthodox opponents.
He switched to a southpaw stance to confuse his opponent.
The southpaw stance allows for different angles of attack.

senses.definition: a stance where a boxer's left hand and left foot are forward, with the right hand and right foot positioned behind

examples.x: He prefers to fight from an orthodox stance to maximize his jab.
The coach instructed him to maintain his balance better in the orthodox stance.
Fighters using the orthodox stance often face southpaws in competitive bouts.
Switching from southpaw to orthodox stance can catch opponents off guard.
His orthodox stance provides a strong foundation for his defensive moves.

senses.definition: the preliminary boxing matches that precede the main event

examples.x: The undercard provided some unexpected knockouts.
He made his professional debut on the undercard last night.
The undercard featured several exciting bouts.
She secured a spot on the undercard after impressive performances in smaller venues.

senses.definition: the standard regulations for modern boxing matches

examples.x: The boxer violated Queensberry rules by hitting below the belt.
Fighters must adhere to Queensberry rules to compete professionally.
Understanding Queensberry rules is essential for aspiring boxers.

senses.definition: a professional boxing match where boxers compete for a cash prize

examples.x: The prizefight ended in a knockout in the early rounds.
Prizefights are broadcasted live on television for fans around the world.
The championship prizefight drew a large audience to the arena.
The referee ensured fair play and sportsmanship throughout the prizefight.
Prizefights are often the culmination of years of training and dedication for boxers.

senses.definition: a push kick used in Muay Thai and other martial arts to create distance or disrupt an opponent's balance

examples.x: The teep kick is a fundamental technique in Muay Thai.
His teep kick disrupted his opponent's rhythm.
He used a teep kick to keep his opponent at bay.
She practiced her teep kick until it was flawless.
The teep kick caught his opponent off guard.

senses.definition: (boxing) a quick maneuver to separate from an opponent, typically from a clinch, aimed at gaining advantage or creating space

examples.x: The boxer used a breakaway to escape the clinch.
She initiated a breakaway to reset the fight.
His breakaway speed helped him evade punches.
The referee called for a breakaway after a prolonged clinch.
He relied on breakaways to control the fight's pace.

senses.definition: a defensive tactic where a boxer responds to an opponent's attack by immediately delivering a punch of their own

examples.x: Her counterpunch stunned her opponent, turning the momentum of the fight.
Counterpunches require precise timing and anticipation of the opponent's moves.
He effectively countered his opponent's jab with a quick counterpunch to the body.
She landed a powerful counterpunch after slipping her opponent's punch.
His opponent's aggression left openings for counterpunches.

senses.definition: an event where individuals compete in the sport of boxing

examples.x: The boxer trained tirelessly for his upcoming bout, determined to win the championship title.
After months of preparation, she finally stepped into the ring for her first professional boxing bout.
The crowd erupted into cheers as the fighters entered the arena for the main event bout.
Despite his injury, the wrestler refused to withdraw from the bout, showing his determination to compete.
